config.source:
SELECT 
  s.order_date,
  s.product_category,
  s.customer_id,
  s.customer_name,
  s.total_price,
  c.region,
  c.state,
  c.city
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id

SELECT
  product_category,
  loyalty_segment,
  total_price
FROM
  databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales
  JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers
  ON sales.customer_id = customers.customer_id

WITH sales_data AS (
  SELECT
    date_format(order_date, "dd") AS day,
    SUM(total_price) AS total_sales
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales
  GROUP BY day
), orders_data AS (
  SELECT
    CASE 
      WHEN try_cast(sales_orders.order_datetime AS BIGINT) IS NOT NULL 
      THEN DAY(FROM_UNIXTIME(sales_orders.order_datetime))
      ELSE NULL 
    END as day,
    COUNT(order_number) AS total_orders
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales_orders
  GROUP BY day
) SELECT
  cast(s.day as INT) as day,
  s.total_sales,
  o.total_orders
FROM sales_data s JOIN orders_data o ON s.day = o.day ORDER BY s.day

WITH customer_orders AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT order_date) as order_frequency
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales
  GROUP BY customer_id
) SELECT 
  order_frequency,
  COUNT(*) as customer_count
FROM customer_orders GROUP BY order_frequency

SELECT 
  c.loyalty_segment,
  s.customer_id,
  SUM(s.total_price) as total_revenue
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY c.loyalty_segment, s.customer_id

SELECT 
  s.customer_id,
  c.customer_name,
  c.loyalty_segment,
  c.region,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.order_date) as total_orders,
  SUM(s.total_price) as lifetime_value,
  AVG(s.total_price) as avg_order_value,
  DATEDIFF(MAX(s.order_date), MIN(s.order_date)) as tenure_days,
  CASE 
    WHEN SUM(s.total_price) >= 200000 THEN 'High Value'
    WHEN SUM(s.total_price) >= 100000 THEN 'Medium Value'
    ELSE 'Low Value'
  END as customer_value_tier
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY s.customer_id, c.customer_name, c.loyalty_segment, c.region

SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', s.order_date) as month,
  c.loyalty_segment,
  s.customer_id,
  SUM(s.total_price) as monthly_revenue
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', s.order_date), c.loyalty_segment, s.customer_id

measures.name: cumulative_revenue
measures.expr: SUM(SUM(monthly_revenue)) OVER (PARTITION BY loyalty_segment ORDER BY month)

config.source:
WITH customer_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    s.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    SUM(s.total_price) as total_revenue
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s
  JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
    ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
  GROUP BY s.customer_id, c.customer_name
), ranked_customers AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    customer_name,
    total_revenue,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY total_revenue DESC) as customer_rank,
    SUM(total_revenue) OVER (ORDER BY total_revenue DESC ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as cumulative_revenue,
    SUM(total_revenue) OVER () as total_revenue_all
  FROM customer_revenue
) SELECT 
  customer_rank,
  customer_id,
  customer_name,
  total_revenue,
  cumulative_revenue,
  cumulative_revenue / total_revenue_all * 100 as cumulative_pct,
  CASE 
    WHEN customer_rank <= CEIL(COUNT(*) OVER () * 0.2) THEN 'Top 20%'
    WHEN customer_rank <= CEIL(COUNT(*) OVER () * 0.5) THEN 'Middle 30%'
    ELSE 'Bottom 50%'
  END as customer_tier
FROM ranked_customers

SELECT 
  c.loyalty_segment,
  c.region,
  s.order_date,
  s.total_price
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id

SELECT 
  c.loyalty_segment,
  s.order_date,
  s.total_price,
  CASE 
    WHEN s.total_price < 5000 THEN 'Under $5K'
    WHEN s.total_price < 10000 THEN '$5K-$10K'
    WHEN s.total_price < 20000 THEN '$10K-$20K'
    WHEN s.total_price < 50000 THEN '$20K-$50K'
    ELSE 'Over $50K'
  END as order_size_bucket
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id

WITH customer_orders AS (
  SELECT 
    s.customer_id,
    c.loyalty_segment,
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.order_date) as order_count
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s
  JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
    ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
  GROUP BY s.customer_id, c.loyalty_segment
) SELECT 
  loyalty_segment,
  customer_id,
  order_count,
  CASE WHEN order_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as is_repeat_customer
FROM customer_orders

SELECT 
  c.loyalty_segment,
  s.customer_id,
  SUM(s.total_price) as customer_revenue
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY c.loyalty_segment, s.customer_id

SELECT 
  DAYOFWEEK(s.order_date) as day_of_week,
  DAY(s.order_date) as day_of_month,
  c.loyalty_segment,
  s.order_date,
  s.total_price
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id

SELECT 
  s.customer_id,
  c.customer_name,
  c.loyalty_segment,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.product_category) as unique_categories_purchased,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.product_name) as unique_products,
  SUM(s.total_price) as total_spent
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY s.customer_id, c.customer_name, c.loyalty_segment

WITH segment_totals AS (
  SELECT 
    c.loyalty_segment,
    SUM(s.total_price) as segment_total_revenue
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s
  JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
    ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
  GROUP BY c.loyalty_segment
) SELECT 
  c.loyalty_segment,
  s.product_category,
  COUNT(*) as purchase_count,
  SUM(s.total_price) as category_revenue,
  st.segment_total_revenue,
  SUM(s.total_price) / st.segment_total_revenue * 100 as pct_of_segment_revenue
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN segment_totals st ON c.loyalty_segment = st.loyalty_segment GROUP BY c.loyalty_segment, s.product_category, st.segment_total_revenue

WITH product_pairs AS (
  SELECT 
    s1.product_category as product_a,
    s2.product_category as product_b,
    s1.customer_id
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s1
  JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s2 
    ON s1.customer_id = s2.customer_id 
    AND s1.product_category < s2.product_category
  GROUP BY s1.product_category, s2.product_category, s1.customer_id
) SELECT 
  product_a,
  product_b,
  COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) as customers_bought_both,
  COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales) as affinity_score
FROM product_pairs GROUP BY product_a, product_b

WITH customer_totals AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    SUM(total_price) as customer_total_spend
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales
  GROUP BY customer_id
) SELECT 
  s.customer_id,
  c.customer_name,
  s.product_category,
  SUM(s.total_price) as category_spend,
  ct.customer_total_spend,
  SUM(s.total_price) / ct.customer_total_spend * 100 as pct_of_customer_spend
FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
  ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN customer_totals ct ON s.customer_id = ct.customer_id GROUP BY s.customer_id, c.customer_name, s.product_category, ct.customer_total_spend

WITH product_rankings AS (
  SELECT 
    c.loyalty_segment,
    s.product_category,
    s.product_name,
    COUNT(*) as purchase_count,
    SUM(s.total_price) as revenue,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.loyalty_segment ORDER BY SUM(s.total_price) DESC) as product_rank
  FROM databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.sales s
  JOIN databricks_simulated_retail_customer_data.v01.customers c 
    ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
  GROUP BY c.loyalty_segment, s.product_category, s.product_name
) SELECT 
  loyalty_segment,
  product_category,
  product_name,
  purchase_count,
  revenue,
  product_rank
FROM product_rankings WHERE product_rank <= 10

frame.title: Avg Order Value by Segment & Region
